mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment typically correlates with economic growth.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent month-over-month growth, yet S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, leading to reduced consumer confidence and corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment is a key indicator of recessionary pressures.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of stagnant or declining retail sales and persistent high inflation indicates a stagflationary environment, where consumer purchasing power is eroded and economic activity slows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth < 0% MoM AND CPIAUCSL growth > 0.3% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflationary periods are historically challenging for equity markets.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth is negative and CPIAUCSL growth is above 0.3% MoM, yet S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of such periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy, which raises borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampens economic activity, and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary tightening typically precedes economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over 6 months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, boosting corporate revenues and investor confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong retail sales are a leading indicator of consumer strength.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth exceeds 0.8% month-over-month, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side weakness, indicating reduced corporate output and profitability, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key measure of economic activity.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 1.5% month-over-month, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year decline in housing starts indicates a severe downturn in the housing market, which impacts consumer wealth, construction activity, and financial stability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market health is a significant driver of economic cycles.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 20% year-over-year, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy, which lowers borrowing costs, stimulates economic activity, and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary easing typically supports asset prices.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines by more than 0.25 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.
